title: [Feature]: Evaluate specialty data warehouse sinks
problem: Oracle GoldenGate connectivity material lists data warehouse targets such as Teradata Vantage, Vertica, IBM Netezza, and related cloud variants. nats-sinks already tracks Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, and Azure Synapse separately, but not these specialty warehouse families.
proposal: Research whether Teradata, Vertica, and IBM Netezza should receive separate sink issues or remain grouped as lower-priority specialty warehouse profiles. The evaluation should cover maintained Python drivers, batch load APIs, staging requirements, transaction boundaries, idempotent merge behavior, and operational complexity.
users: Enterprise warehouse teams operating Teradata, Vertica, Netezza, or compatible systems.
delivery_semantics: Each accepted warehouse target must define a durable commit boundary before it can be ACK-gated. Staged load jobs must be confirmed before success is returned.
security: Warehouse credentials, hostnames, schemas, table names, staging locations, and payloads must be redacted. SQL must use parameters or safe load manifests.
acceptance: Research ranks Teradata, Vertica, and Netezza demand and feasibility.
Separate implementation issues are created for any accepted sink.
The evaluation documents idempotency and staging patterns.
No implementation starts from this grouped issue.
Documentation explains why specialty warehouses are handled separately from generic JDBC.
tests: Research-only initially. Future sinks require mocked driver/load tests and gated integration tests.
documentation: Add findings to future sink roadmap documentation.
closeout: Close after the evaluation release and follow-up issues are created.
